1	# Plan 001: Build the Stage 0 observed job review
     2	
     3	> **Executor instructions:** You are the sole implementation agent for this plan.
     4	> Use the exact `deepseek-v4-pro` model. Follow every step in order. Run each
     5	> verification command and confirm its expected result before moving on. If a
     6	> STOP condition occurs, stop and report it. Do not improvise, add adjacent
     7	> capabilities, or preserve old code through compatibility wrappers.
     8	>
     9	> **Reviewer instructions:** Implementation is reviewed by three independent
    10	> `deepseek-v4-flash` agents after the executor produces one clean candidate
    11	> commit. Reviewers are read-only. They report findings and never edit code.
    12	> The Pro executor fixes accepted findings. No other model may substitute for
    13	> either role without Arinze's explicit approval.
    14	
    15	## Status
    16	
    17	- **Priority:** P1
    18	- **Effort:** L
    19	- **Risk:** MED
    20	- **Depends on:** none, but execution is blocked by the baseline gate below
    21	- **Category:** direction, architecture, tests
    22	- **Planned at:** commit `5423187`, 2026-07-17
    23	- **Working-tree basis:** the intentional dirty greenfield cleanup and the untracked `discussion/` and `diagram/` trees present on 2026-07-17
    24	- **Current status:** BLOCKED until that cleanup and the discussion/diagram sources are committed as one reviewed baseline, then this plan is restamped to the new commit
    25	
    26	## Baseline gate, run before dispatch
    27	
    28	This plan was written while the repository had broad uncommitted cleanup work. An isolated executor worktree created from `5423187` would not contain the discussion notes, fixtures, diagrams, test deletions, or documentation state used to write this plan.
    29	
    30	The advisor or operator must first:
    31	
    32	1. Review and commit the intentional cleanup plus `discussion/`, `diagram/`, and `docs/harness-primitives/` work.
    33	2. Leave the main checkout clean.
    34	3. Update this plan's `Planned at` SHA and every drift command to that commit.
    35	4. Confirm the files listed below match the recorded design.
    36	5. Confirm the subagent runtime can call the exact models `deepseek-v4-pro` and `deepseek-v4-flash`.
    37	
    38	Run:
    39	
    40	```bash
    41	test -z "$(git status --porcelain=v1 --untracked-files=all)"
    42	git rev-parse --short HEAD
    43	test -f discussion/004-minimal-job-native-harness.md
    44	test -f discussion/005-implementation-stack-observability-and-verification.md
    45	test -f discussion/006-pi-inspired-haxjobs-architecture.md
    46	test -f discussion/research/2026-07-17-pi-hermes-job-native-harness-study.md
    47	test -f discussion/fixtures/003-five-job-sample.md
    48	test -f diagram/README.md
    49	```
    50	
    51	Expected: every command exits 0, the status output is empty, and the current commit is recorded into this plan before execution.
    52	
    53	Then run the updated drift check:
    54	
    55	```bash
    56	git diff --stat <PLANNED_SHA>..HEAD -- \
    57	  pyproject.toml uv.lock src/haxjobs/cli.py \
    58	  src/haxjobs/model src/haxjobs/agent_core \
    59	  src/haxjobs/employment src/haxjobs/interfaces \
    60	  tests discussion/006-pi-inspired-haxjobs-architecture.md \
    61	  discussion/fixtures diagram docs/implementation-reports
    62	```
    63	
    64	Expected: no output.
    65	
    66	**STOP:** do not dispatch an executor while this plan still says `<PLANNED_SHA>`, while the main checkout is dirty, or when either exact DeepSeek model is unavailable. Do not silently fall back to another model.
    67	
    68	## Why this matters
    69	
    70	HaxJobs currently has an old single-turn agent and an old tool loop, but the greenfield design says to learn from one real job-review run before building sessions, memory, workflows, or a large tool catalogue.
    71	
    72	This plan creates the smallest observable Hax:
    73	
    74	```text
    75	CLI
    76	-> employment job-review context
    77	-> generic no-tool agent core
    78	-> one provider adapter
    79	-> local private run artifacts
    80	-> human review
    81	```
    82	
    83	Job 49 tests whether Hax can reject a technically noisy but wrong career direction. Job 328 tests whether Hax stays honest when the stored vacancy is only a title and URL.
    84	
    85	## Decisions this plan implements
    86	
    87	The executor has zero access to this conversation, so these decisions are repeated here.
    88	
    89	### Decided experiment
    90	
    91	From `discussion/004-minimal-job-native-harness.md`:
    92	
    93	- freeze one backend-career fixture from the user's real evidence
    94	- review Job 49 first
    95	- review Job 328 second
    96	- Stage 0 has no tools
    97	- use one configured real model and one scripted fake
    98	- save each run and complete a human review
    99	- do not build sessions, compaction, databases, skills, sub-agents, schedulers, company watches, or UI
   100	
   101	### Decided stack
   102	
   103	From `discussion/005-implementation-stack-observability-and-verification.md`:
   104	
   105	- Python owns Hax and employment logic
   106	- Pydantic v2 validates model-facing and trust-boundary data
   107	- use plain Python rather than an agent framework
   108	- use `uv`
   109	- use `argparse`
   110	- use local redacted JSONL, Python logging, pytest, and Markdown human review
   111	- PydanticAI is the first framework to reconsider only after meaningful loop code accumulates
   112	
   113	### Architecture direction settled by the current request
   114	
   115	For this implementation wave, use Pi's conceptual split inside one Python distribution:
   116	
   117	1. `haxjobs.model`: provider and normalized model boundary
   118	2. `haxjobs.agent_core`: domain-free messages, events, run result, and model-call lifecycle
   119	3. `haxjobs.employment`: Hax instructions, career/job fixture contracts, and job-review context
   120	4. `haxjobs.interfaces`: thin CLI-facing adapter
   121	
   122	Do not create four distributions or a package monorepo.
   123	
   124	This plan does not decide the future document-workspace, coding-workspace, or shell boundaries from `discussion/006`. Those remain later work.
   125	
   126	## Current state
   127	
   128	### Repository verification baseline
   129	
   130	At planning time:
   131	
   132	```text
   133	PYTHONPATH=src:. .venv/bin/python -m py_compile $(find src/haxjobs cron -name '*.py')
   134	-> PASS
   135	
   136	PYTHONPATH=src:. .venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/
   137	-> ERROR: file or directory not found: tests/
   138	
   139	uv lock --check
   140	-> PASS
   141	```
   142	
   143	The intentional cleanup removed the old test tree. This plan establishes the first greenfield test floor.
   144	
   145	### Current dependency declaration
   146	
   147	`pyproject.toml` declares FastAPI, Uvicorn, Markdown, OpenAI, and multipart support. Pydantic 2.13.4 is currently installed transitively through FastAPI but is not a direct project dependency.
   148	
   149	The new code imports Pydantic directly, so `pyproject.toml` must declare `pydantic>=2.13,<3` and `uv.lock` must record it.
   150	
   151	### Current agent coupling to avoid
   152	
   153	`src/haxjobs/agent/agent.py` currently owns provider config, OpenAI client creation, provider message dictionaries, one-call execution, and the tool loop in one class.
   154	
   155	`src/haxjobs/agent/registry.py` stores a process-global tool catalogue and dispatches by name without receiving the active set for the current run.
   156	
   157	Do not extend or import these modules from the greenfield experiment. Two runtimes may coexist temporarily, but there must be no adapter, re-export, or compatibility wrapper between them.
   158	
   159	### Fixture facts
   160	
   161	`discussion/fixtures/003-five-job-sample.md` is the source for both machine fixtures.
   162	
   163	Job 49:
   164	
   165	- Trainline IT Support Analyst
   166	- substantial but truncated 5,000-character source snapshot
   167	- Tier 2 internal IT support duties
   168	- wrong broad direction for the user's backend and AI track
   169	- sponsorship is not proven by the stored vacancy evidence
   170	
   171	Job 328:
   172	
   173	- Oritain Software Engineer, Mid-Level, Full Stack
   174	- stored evidence is only a title and LinkedIn URL stub
   175	- old evaluation details must remain hidden from Hax
   176	- Stage 0 should refuse a confident fit judgement and name source inspection as the next useful step
   177	
   178	## Commands you will need
   179	
   180	Run from the repository root.
   181	
   182	| Purpose | Command | Expected result |
   183	|---|---|---|
   184	| Lock check | `uv lock --check` | exit 0 |
   185	| Focused tests | `PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run python3 -m pytest -q tests/test_stage0_job_review.py` | all tests pass, no network |
   186	| All greenfield tests | `PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run python3 -m pytest -q tests/` | all tests pass |
   187	| Compile | `PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run python3 -m py_compile $(find src/haxjobs/model src/haxjobs/agent_core src/haxjobs/employment src/haxjobs/interfaces tests -name '*.py')` | exit 0 |
   188	| CLI help | `PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run haxjobs experiment review-job --help` | exit 0 and documented options |
   189	| Fake run | `PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run haxjobs experiment review-job --job 49 --fake` | exit 0, prints answer, run ID, artifact path, review path |
   190	| Diff check | `git diff --check` | no output |
   191	
   192	Do not run frontend commands. The frontend is intentionally removed in the current greenfield cleanup and is outside this plan.
   193	
   194	## Suggested executor toolkit
   195	
   196	- Read `.agents/skills/readable-code/SKILL.md` before writing Python.
   197	- Read `.agents/skills/clean-drawio/SKILL.md` before creating the diagram.
   198	- Use standard-library `argparse`, `logging`, `hashlib`, `json`, `pathlib`, `tomllib`, `uuid`, and UTC datetimes where they are enough.
   199	- Use the installed OpenAI-compatible SDK only in the provider adapter.
   200	- Do not add Typer, Click, HTTPX, requests, PydanticAI, LangChain, OpenTelemetry, Phoenix, or another package.
   201	
   202	## Scope
   203	
   204	### In scope
   205	
   206	Existing files that may change:
   207	
   208	- `pyproject.toml`
   209	- `uv.lock`
   210	- `src/haxjobs/cli.py`
   211	- `discussion/006-pi-inspired-haxjobs-architecture.md`

   213	
   214	New tracked files:
   215	
   216	- `src/haxjobs/model/__init__.py`

1	# Plan 001: Build the Stage 0 observed job review
     2	
     3	> **Executor instructions:** You are the sole implementation agent for this plan.
     4	> Use the exact `deepseek-v4-pro` model. Follow every step in order. Run each
     5	> verification command and confirm its expected result before moving on. If a
     6	> STOP condition occurs, stop and report it. Do not improvise, add adjacent
     7	> capabilities, or preserve old code through compatibility wrappers.
     8	>
     9	> **Reviewer instructions:** Implementation is reviewed by three independent
    10	> `deepseek-v4-flash` agents after the executor produces one clean candidate
    11	> commit. Reviewers are read-only. They report findings and never edit code.
    12	> The Pro executor fixes accepted findings. No other model may substitute for
    13	> either role without Arinze's explicit approval.
    14	
    15	## Status
    16	
    17	- **Priority:** P1
    18	- **Effort:** L
    19	- **Risk:** MED
    20	- **Depends on:** none, but execution is blocked by the baseline gate below
    21	- **Category:** direction, architecture, tests
    22	- **Planned at:** commit `5423187`, 2026-07-17
    23	- **Working-tree basis:** the intentional dirty greenfield cleanup and the untracked `discussion/` and `diagram/` trees present on 2026-07-17
    24	- **Current status:** BLOCKED until that cleanup and the discussion/diagram sources are committed as one reviewed baseline, then this plan is restamped to the new commit
    25	
    26	## Baseline gate, run before dispatch
    27	
    28	This plan was written while the repository had broad uncommitted cleanup work. An isolated executor worktree created from `5423187` would not contain the discussion notes, fixtures, diagrams, test deletions, or documentation state used to write this plan.
    29	
    30	The advisor or operator must first:
    31	
    32	1. Review and commit the intentional cleanup plus `discussion/`, `diagram/`, and `docs/harness-primitives/` work.
    33	2. Leave the main checkout clean.
    34	3. Update this plan's `Planned at` SHA and every drift command to that commit.
    35	4. Confirm the files listed below match the recorded design.
    36	5. Confirm the subagent runtime can call the exact models `deepseek-v4-pro` and `deepseek-v4-flash`.
    37	
    38	Run:
    39	
    40	```bash
    41	test -z "$(git status --porcelain=v1 --untracked-files=all)"
    42	git rev-parse --short HEAD
    43	test -f discussion/004-minimal-job-native-harness.md
    44	test -f discussion/005-implementation-stack-observability-and-verification.md
    45	test -f discussion/006-pi-inspired-haxjobs-architecture.md
    46	test -f discussion/research/2026-07-17-pi-hermes-job-native-harness-study.md
    47	test -f discussion/fixtures/003-five-job-sample.md
    48	test -f diagram/README.md
    49	```
    50	
    51	Expected: every command exits 0, the status output is empty, and the current commit is recorded into this plan before execution.
    52	
    53	Then run the updated drift check:
    54	
    55	```bash
    56	git diff --stat <PLANNED_SHA>..HEAD -- \
    57	  pyproject.toml uv.lock src/haxjobs/cli.py \
    58	  src/haxjobs/model src/haxjobs/agent_core \
    59	  src/haxjobs/employment src/haxjobs/interfaces \
    60	  tests discussion/006-pi-inspired-haxjobs-architecture.md \
    61	  discussion/fixtures diagram docs/implementation-reports
    62	```
    63	
    64	Expected: no output.
    65	
    66	**STOP:** do not dispatch an executor while this plan still says `<PLANNED_SHA>`, while the main checkout is dirty, or when either exact DeepSeek model is unavailable. Do not silently fall back to another model.
    67	
    68	## Why this matters
    69	
    70	HaxJobs currently has an old single-turn agent and an old tool loop, but the greenfield design says to learn from one real job-review run before building sessions, memory, workflows, or a large tool catalogue.
    71	
    72	This plan creates the smallest observable Hax:
    73	
    74	```text
    75	CLI
    76	-> employment job-review context
    77	-> generic no-tool agent core
    78	-> one provider adapter
    79	-> local private run artifacts
    80	-> human review
    81	```
    82	
    83	Job 49 tests whether Hax can reject a technically noisy but wrong career direction. Job 328 tests whether Hax stays honest when the stored vacancy is only a title and URL.
    84	
    85	## Decisions this plan implements
    86	
    87	The executor has zero access to this conversation, so these decisions are repeated here.
    88	
    89	### Decided experiment
    90	
    91	From `discussion/004-minimal-job-native-harness.md`:
    92	
    93	- freeze one backend-career fixture from the user's real evidence
    94	- review Job 49 first
    95	- review Job 328 second
    96	- Stage 0 has no tools
    97	- use one configured real model and one scripted fake
    98	- save each run and complete a human review
    99	- do not build sessions, compaction, databases, skills, sub-agents, schedulers, company watches, or UI
   100	
   101	### Decided stack
   102	
   103	From `discussion/005-implementation-stack-observability-and-verification.md`:
   104	
   105	- Python owns Hax and employment logic
   106	- Pydantic v2 validates model-facing and trust-boundary data
   107	- use plain Python rather than an agent framework
   108	- use `uv`
   109	- use `argparse`
   110	- use local redacted JSONL, Python logging, pytest, and Markdown human review
   111	- PydanticAI is the first framework to reconsider only after meaningful loop code accumulates
   112	
   113	### Architecture direction settled by the current request
   114	
   115	For this implementation wave, use Pi's conceptual split inside one Python distribution:
   116	
   117	1. `haxjobs.model`: provider and normalized model boundary
   118	2. `haxjobs.agent_core`: domain-free messages, events, run result, and model-call lifecycle
   119	3. `haxjobs.employment`: Hax instructions, career/job fixture contracts, and job-review context
   120	4. `haxjobs.interfaces`: thin CLI-facing adapter
   121	
   122	Do not create four distributions or a package monorepo.
   123	
   124	This plan does not decide the future document-workspace, coding-workspace, or shell boundaries from `discussion/006`. Those remain later work.
   125	
   126	## Current state
   127	
   128	### Repository verification baseline
   129	
   130	At planning time:
   131	
   132	```text
   133	PYTHONPATH=src:. .venv/bin/python -m py_compile $(find src/haxjobs cron -name '*.py')
   134	-> PASS
   135	
   136	PYTHONPATH=src:. .venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/
   137	-> ERROR: file or directory not found: tests/
   138	
   139	uv lock --check
   140	-> PASS
   141	```
   142	
   143	The intentional cleanup removed the old test tree. This plan establishes the first greenfield test floor.
   144	
   145	### Current dependency declaration
   146	
   147	`pyproject.toml` declares FastAPI, Uvicorn, Markdown, OpenAI, and multipart support. Pydantic 2.13.4 is currently installed transitively through FastAPI but is not a direct project dependency.
   148	
   149	The new code imports Pydantic directly, so `pyproject.toml` must declare `pydantic>=2.13,<3` and `uv.lock` must record it.
   150	
   151	### Current agent coupling to avoid
   152	
   153	`src/haxjobs/agent/agent.py` currently owns provider config, OpenAI client creation, provider message dictionaries, one-call execution, and the tool loop in one class.
   154	
   155	`src/haxjobs/agent/registry.py` stores a process-global tool catalogue and dispatches by name without receiving the active set for the current run.
   156	
   157	Do not extend or import these modules from the greenfield experiment. Two runtimes may coexist temporarily, but there must be no adapter, re-export, or compatibility wrapper between them.
   158	
   159	### Fixture facts
   160	
   161	`discussion/fixtures/003-five-job-sample.md` is the source for both machine fixtures.
   162	
   163	Job 49:
   164	
   165	- Trainline IT Support Analyst
   166	- substantial but truncated 5,000-character source snapshot
   167	- Tier 2 internal IT support duties
   168	- wrong broad direction for the user's backend and AI track
   169	- sponsorship is not proven by the stored vacancy evidence
   170	
   171	Job 328:
   172	
   173	- Oritain Software Engineer, Mid-Level, Full Stack
   174	- stored evidence is only a title and LinkedIn URL stub
   175	- old evaluation details must remain hidden from Hax
   176	- Stage 0 should refuse a confident fit judgement and name source inspection as the next useful step
   177	
   178	## Commands you will need
   179	
   180	Run from the repository root.
   181	
   182	| Purpose | Command | Expected result |
   183	|---|---|---|
   184	| Lock check | `uv lock --check` | exit 0 |
   185	| Focused tests | `PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run python3 -m pytest -q tests/test_stage0_job_review.py` | all tests pass, no network |
   186	| All greenfield tests | `PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run python3 -m pytest -q tests/` | all tests pass |
   187	| Compile | `PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run python3 -m py_compile $(find src/haxjobs/model src/haxjobs/agent_core src/haxjobs/employment src/haxjobs/interfaces tests -name '*.py')` | exit 0 |
   188	| CLI help | `PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run haxjobs experiment review-job --help` | exit 0 and documented options |
   189	| Fake run | `PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run haxjobs experiment review-job --job 49 --fake` | exit 0, prints answer, run ID, artifact path, review path |
   190	| Diff check | `git diff --check` | no output |
   191	
   192	Do not run frontend commands. The frontend is intentionally removed in the current greenfield cleanup and is outside this plan.
   193	
   194	## Suggested executor toolkit
   195	
   196	- Read `.agents/skills/readable-code/SKILL.md` before writing Python.
   197	- Read `.agents/skills/clean-drawio/SKILL.md` before creating the diagram.
   198	- Use standard-library `argparse`, `logging`, `hashlib`, `json`, `pathlib`, `tomllib`, `uuid`, and UTC datetimes where they are enough.
   199	- Use the installed OpenAI-compatible SDK only in the provider adapter.
   200	- Do not add Typer, Click, HTTPX, requests, PydanticAI, LangChain, OpenTelemetry, Phoenix, or another package.
   201	
   202	## Scope
   203	
   204	### In scope
   205	
   206	Existing files that may change:
   207	
   208	- `pyproject.toml`
   209	- `uv.lock`
   210	- `src/haxjobs/cli.py`

   213

   269	
   270	## Git and execution workflow
   271	
   272	### Branch and worktree
   273	
   274	- Execute in an isolated worktree from the clean, restamped baseline.
   275	- Branch: `advisor/001-stage0-observed-job-review`
   276	- Sole writer: `deepseek-v4-pro`
   277	- Commit in logical units. Match the repository's plain sentence commit style, for example: `Build Stage 0 observed job review`.
   278	- Do not push, merge, or open a pull request unless the operator asks.
   279	
   280	### Model preflight
   281	
   282	Before the executor edits anything:
   283	
   284	1. Resolve the exact model ID `deepseek-v4-pro`.
   285	2. Make one minimal no-tool health call.
   286	3. Resolve `deepseek-v4-flash` and make one minimal health call for the later reviewers.
   287	4. Record only model ID, timestamp, success or safe failure category, and a provider request ID if available.
   288	5. Never print or copy provider credentials.
   289	
   290	**STOP:** if either exact model is unavailable, unauthorized, mapped to another model, or rate-disabled. No substitution.
   291	
   292	## Steps
   293	
   294	### Step 1: Record the accepted implementation boundary
   295	
   296	Update only the decision ledger and status text in `discussion/006-pi-inspired-haxjobs-architecture.md`.
   297	
   298	Record that this implementation request accepts:
   299	
   300	- Pi's conceptual model, agent-core, employment-layer, and interface split
   301	- one Python distribution for now
   302	- Stage 0 and Stage 1 as the first experiment sequence
   303	
   304	Keep the document-workspace, coding-workspace, and bash questions open. Do not rewrite the technical study.
   305	
   306	**Verify:**
   307	
   308	```bash
   309	rg -n "status:|Pi as architecture reference|Physical package split|Coding tools in HaxJobs|Document file tools|Bash" discussion/006-pi-inspired-haxjobs-architecture.md
   310	```
   311	
   312	Expected: the four-layer and one-package choices are clearly decided; the three future workspace or shell items remain open.
   313	
   314	### Step 2: Declare Pydantic as a direct dependency
   315	
   316	Add `pydantic>=2.13,<3` to project dependencies and update `uv.lock` using `uv lock` or `uv add`.
   317	
   318	Do not change the Python version or any unrelated dependency.
   319	
   320	**Verify:**
   321	
   322	```bash
   323	uv lock --check
   324	uv run python3 -c "import pydantic; assert pydantic.VERSION.startswith('2.')"
   325	```
   326	
   327	Expected: both commands exit 0.
   328	
   329	### Step 3: Create machine-readable, source-limited fixtures
   330	
   331	Create `discussion/fixtures/harness/job-49.json` and `job-328.json` from `discussion/fixtures/003-five-job-sample.md`.
   332	
   333	Required common fields:
   334	
   335	```text
   336	fixture_id
   337	fixture_version
   338	job_ref
   339	observed_at
   340	source_type
   341	source_url
   342	source_status
   343	title
   344	employer_name
   345	location
   346	description
   347	content_complete
   348	warnings
   349	```
   350	
   351	Rules:
   352	
   353	- Job 49 contains the preserved 5,000-character description and `content_complete=false`.
   354	- Job 49 warning says the source snapshot ends mid-description.
   355	- Job 328 contains only the title and source URL stub. Its description must not contain old evaluation details.
   356	- Job 328 uses `source_status="lead_only"` or an equally explicit bounded vocabulary.
   357	- Neither fixture contains old fit scores, fit verdicts, recommended CV variants, old sponsorship risk labels, cycle reports, or model-written evaluation prose.
   358	- Do not infer employer, stack, sponsorship, salary, work mode, or seniority beyond the stored evidence.
   359	
   360	Create `discussion/fixtures/harness/job-review-rubric.md` with the Job 49, Job 328, engineering, privacy, and voice checks from `discussion/004` and `discussion/005`.
   361	
   362	Create `tests/fixtures/job_review/career.json` as a synthetic, non-private test profile. It must resemble the contract but must not copy private contact details or local paths.
   363	
   364	**Verify:**
   365	
   366	```bash
   367	python3 - <<'PY'
   368	import json
   369	from pathlib import Path
   370	for name in ("job-49.json", "job-328.json"):
   371	    data = json.loads((Path("discussion/fixtures/harness") / name).read_text())
   372	    required = {
   373	        "fixture_id", "fixture_version", "job_ref", "observed_at",
   374	        "source_type", "source_url", "source_status", "title",
   375	        "employer_name", "location", "description",
   376	        "content_complete", "warnings",
   377	    }
   378	    assert required <= data.keys(), (name, required - data.keys())
   379	job328 = json.loads(Path("discussion/fixtures/harness/job-328.json").read_text())
   380	assert len(job328["description"]) <= 250
   381	assert job328["content_complete"] is False
   382	print("fixture contracts: PASS")
   383	PY
   384	```
   385	
   386	Expected: `fixture contracts: PASS`.
   387	
   388	### Step 4: Create the model boundary
   389	
   390	Build `src/haxjobs/model/` without importing any employment or legacy-agent module.
   391	
   392	#### Contracts in `model/types.py`
   393	
   394	Use Pydantic models for:
   395	
   396	- `ModelMessage`: role plus text content for Stage 0
   397	- `ModelRequest`: stable system text, ordered internal messages, model settings
   398	- `ModelUsage`: optional input, output, and total token counts
   399	- `ModelResponse`: final text, provider, exact model, stop reason, optional usage
   400	- `ModelFailure`: safe category and message with no raw provider body
   401	
   402	Keep provider-specific raw objects out of these contracts.
   403	
   404	#### Protocol and adapter in `model/client.py`
   405	
   406	Define an async `ModelClient` protocol:
   407	
   408	```python
   409	class ModelClient(Protocol):
   410	    async def complete(self, request: ModelRequest) -> ModelResponse: ...
   411	```
   412	
   413	Implement one OpenAI-compatible adapter using `AsyncOpenAI`.
   414	
   415	Read provider configuration from `~/.haxjobs/haxjobs.toml` using `tomllib`, or from the already documented environment variable types. Keep this parser in the model boundary. Do not import `haxjobs.features.setup.service` because that would couple the greenfield runtime to the old web setup flow.
   416	
   417	The adapter must:
   418	
   419	- require configured base URL, model, and credential
   420	- never log credential values or authorization headers
   421	- accept timeout, temperature, and max-token settings
   422	- map the provider response into `ModelResponse`
   423	- turn expected provider failures into a safe typed failure at the boundary
   424	- use no fallback provider or model
   425	
   426	#### Scripted fake in `model/fake.py`
   427	
   428	The fake receives an ordered list of responses or failures, records requests, and returns the next item. It must never call the network.
   429	
   430	**Verify:**
   431	
   432	```bash
   433	PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run python3 - <<'PY'
   434	import sys
   435	from haxjobs.model.fake import FakeModelClient
   436	assert "haxjobs.agent" not in sys.modules
   437	print("model boundary: PASS")
   438	PY
   439	```
   440	
   441	Expected: `model boundary: PASS`.
   442	
   443	### Step 5: Create the domain-free Stage 0 agent core
   444	
   445	Build `src/haxjobs/agent_core/`.
   446	
   447	#### Internal contracts in `agent_core/types.py`
   448	
   449	Define:
   450	
   451	- `AgentMessage`
   452	- `RunRequest`
   453	- `RunResult`
   454	- `RunExitReason`
   455	
   456	Stage 0 needs only user and assistant text. Keep the internal message type separate from provider `ModelMessage`, even when their first fields look similar.
   457	
   458	`RunResult` must carry:
   459	
   460	- run ID
   461	- final text or safe failure
   462	- exit reason
   463	- model/provider metadata
   464	- duration
   465	- optional usage
   466	- artifact directory
   467	- observer errors
   468	
   469	#### Events in `agent_core/events.py`
   470

   329	### Step 3: Create machine-readable, source-limited fixtures

   352

   354	- Job 49 warning says the source snapshot ends mid-description.

   388	### Step 4: Create the model boundary
   389	
   390	Build `src/haxjobs/model/` without importing any employment or legacy-agent module.
   391	
   392	#### Contracts in `model/types.py`
   393	
   394	Use Pydantic models for:
   395	
   396	- `ModelMessage`: role plus text content for Stage 0
   397	- `ModelRequest`: stable system text, ordered internal messages, model settings
   398	- `ModelUsage`: optional input, output, and total token counts
   399	- `ModelResponse`: final text, provider, exact model, stop reason, optional usage
   400	- `ModelFailure`: safe category and message with no raw provider body
   401	
   402	Keep provider-specific raw objects out of these contracts.
   403	
   404	#### Protocol and adapter in `model/client.py`
   405	
   406	Define an async `ModelClient` protocol:
   407	
   408	```python
   409	class ModelClient(Protocol):
   410	    async def complete(self, request: ModelRequest) -> ModelResponse: ...
   411	```
   412	
   413	Implement one OpenAI-compatible adapter using `AsyncOpenAI`.
   414	
   415	Read provider configuration from `~/.haxjobs/haxjobs.toml` using `tomllib`, or from the already documented environment variable types. Keep this parser in the model boundary. Do not import `haxjobs.features.setup.service` because that would couple the greenfield runtime to the old web setup flow.
   416	
   417	The adapter must:
   418	
   419	- require configured base URL, model, and credential
   420	- never log credential values or authorization headers
   421	- accept timeout, temperature, and max-token settings
   422	- map the provider response into `ModelResponse`
   423	- turn expected provider failures into a safe typed failure at the boundary
   424	- use no fallback provider or model
   425	
   426	#### Scripted fake in `model/fake.py`
   427	
   428	The fake receives an ordered list of responses or failures, records requests, and returns the next item. It must never call the network.

   437	print("model boundary: PASS")

   439	```

   442	
   443	### Step 5: Create the domain-free Stage 0 agent core
   444	
   445	Build `src/haxjobs/agent_core/`.
   446	
   447	#### Internal contracts in `agent_core/types.py`
   448	
   449	Define:
   450	
   451	- `AgentMessage`
   452	- `RunRequest`
   453	- `RunResult`
   454	- `RunExitReason`
   455	
   456	Stage 0 needs only user and assistant text. Keep the internal message type separate from provider `ModelMessage`, even when their first fields look similar.
   457	
   458	`RunResult` must carry:
   459	
   460	- run ID
   461	- final text or safe failure
   462	- exit reason
   463	- model/provider metadata
   464	- duration
   465	- optional usage
   466	- artifact directory
   467	- observer errors
   468	
   469	#### Events in `agent_core/events.py`
   470

text:
470	
   471	Define a versioned Pydantic event envelope and an observer protocol.
   472	
   473	Stage 0 event order:
   474	
   475	```text
   476	run_started
   477	context_prepared
   478	model_started
   479	model_completed OR model_failed
   480	run_completed OR run_failed
   481	```
   482	
   483	Events contain safe IDs, timestamps, durations, hashes, counts, provider/model names, and safe error categories. They exclude raw prompts, career data, model text, provider bodies, credentials, headers, and local private paths.
   484	
   485	Observer failures are logged and collected in `RunResult.observer_errors`. They do not change the model result.
   486	
   487	#### Artifact store in `agent_core/artifacts.py`
   488	
   489	Default root: `state/harness-runs`.
   490	
   491	Create each run directory with mode `0700`. Write `context.json` and `transcript.json` with mode `0600` through atomic temp-file replacement. Keep exact selected context and transcript local and ignored by git.
   492	
   493	Files per run:
   494	
   495	- `events.jsonl`: redacted lifecycle events
   496	- `manifest.json`: versions, hashes, fixture IDs, provider/model settings, app commit when available
   497	- `context.json`: exact bounded selected context, local only
   498	- `transcript.json`: exact bounded messages, local only
   499	- `result.json`: structured run result
   500	- `review.md`: copied human rubric with run metadata
   501	
   502	#### One-call runtime in `agent_core/runtime.py`
   503	
   504	The runtime receives a frozen `RunRequest`, a `ModelClient`, and observers. It:
   505	
   506	1. emits start and context events
   507	2. projects internal messages to `ModelRequest`
   508	3. performs exactly one awaited model call
   509	4. maps the response or failure
   510	5. writes local receipts
   511	6. emits completion
   512	7. returns `RunResult`
   513	
   514	Do not add a loop, tool registry, retry, streaming, session, compaction, steering, follow-up, cancellation system, save-point system, or database.
   515	
   516	**Verify:** focused tests added in Step 8 must prove one model call and exact event order.
   517	
   518	### Step 6: Build the employment-layer job review
   519	
   520	Build `src/haxjobs/employment/`.
   521	
   522	#### Fixture contracts in `employment/fixtures.py`
   523	
   524	Define Pydantic contracts for:
   525	
   526	- machine job fixture
   527	- frozen career fixture
   528	- source-labelled evidence item
   529	- career direction and hard constraints
   530	
   531	Reject fixtures missing source, observation date, or evidence provenance.
   532	
   533	The local private fixture at `state/experiments/fixtures/backend-career.json` must contain only the selected backend direction and evidence needed for these two jobs:
   534	
   535	- target role direction
   536	- acceptable location/work mode where relevant
   537	- sponsorship requirement as user-stated truth
   538	- a small list of relevant evidence claims with source type and evidence strength
   539	- fixture creation date and version
   540	
   541	Exclude name, email, phone, full address, credential values, unrelated career tracks, raw CV text, and private local paths. Use opaque local source references where provenance must remain local.
   542	
   543	#### Prompt and context assembly in `employment/review_job.py`
   544	
   545	Keep two stable instruction strings:
   546	
   547	1. Hax identity and truth rules
   548	2. job-review flow
   549	
   550	They must implement the short hypotheses in `discussion/004`, including:
   551	
   552	- Hax helps the user get interviews and become more employable
   553	- speak naturally and directly
   554	- do not invent user, job, company, sponsorship, or currentness claims
   555	- distinguish supported facts, user statements, inference, and unknowns
   556	- check hard constraints before softer fit
   557	- explain strongest overlap, blockers, and important unknowns
   558	- if evidence is insufficient, say what should be checked next
   559	- return a natural answer, not a scorecard
   560	
   561	Build four labelled volatile blocks in fixed order:
   562	
   563	1. user request
   564	2. career direction and constraints
   565	3. relevant evidence
   566	4. job source snapshot
   567	
   568	Do not include the old evaluation, whole profile, whole database row, unrelated projects, or company research.
   569	
   570	The employment host creates the frozen `RunRequest` and calls the agent core. It contains no provider-specific dictionaries and no artifact-writing implementation.
   571	
   572	**Verify:** fixture and context tests in Step 8.
   573	
   574	### Step 7: Add the thin experiment CLI
   575	
   576	Create `src/haxjobs/interfaces/experiment_cli.py` and register a new sibling command in `src/haxjobs/cli.py`:
   577	
   578	```text
   579	haxjobs experiment review-job
   580	```
   581	
   582	Arguments:
   583	
   584	- `--job {49,328}`
   585	- mutually exclusive required choice: `--fake` or `--live`
   586	- `--career-fixture`, default `state/experiments/fixtures/backend-career.json`
   587	- `--artifacts-dir`, default `state/harness-runs`
   588	- optional model settings already supported by the adapter, with conservative defaults
   589	
   590	Rules:
   591	
   592	- `--fake` uses a clearly labelled canned scripted response and no network.
   593	- `--live` uses the configured provider and performs exactly one model call.
   594	- no implicit live call
   595	- no provider fallback
   596	- print final answer, run ID, artifact directory, and review path
   597	- print safe failure categories to stderr and return non-zero
   598	- if observer/artifact errors occur, preserve the answer but exit non-zero because the experiment receipt is incomplete
   599	- do not modify `haxjobs agent ask`
   600	- keep all employment logic outside argparse handlers
   601	
   602	**Verify:**
   603	
   604	```bash
   605	PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run haxjobs experiment review-job --help
   606	PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run haxjobs experiment review-job --job 49 --fake
   607	```
   608	
   609	Expected: both exit 0; the fake run creates one complete ignored run directory.
   610	
   611	### Step 8: Establish the greenfield deterministic test floor
   612	
   613	Create `tests/test_stage0_job_review.py`. Keep helpers local to the test file unless a second test file later proves sharing is useful.
   614	
   615	Required tests:
   616	
   617	1. Job fixture contracts accept Job 49 and Job 328.
   618	2. Missing source/date/provenance is rejected.
   619	3. Job 49 is marked truncated.
   620	4. Job 328 remains a title/URL stub and contains no old evaluation claims.
   621	5. Context block order is fixed and source-labelled.
   622	6. Scripted fake performs exactly one call.
   623	7. The core exposes zero tools and sends no tool schema.
   624	8. Event order matches the Stage 0 contract.
   625	9. JSONL excludes raw fixture content, model text, credentials, headers, and private fixture path.
   626	10. Context and transcript files remain local, bounded, and mode `0600` on POSIX.
   627	11. Run directory mode is `0700` on POSIX.
   628	12. Deterministic manifest hash is stable for the same fixture and prompt versions.
   629	13. Provider failure returns a safe failed `RunResult` and complete failure events.
   630	14. Observer failure is recorded without changing model output.
   631	15. CLI fake run exits 0 and creates all six receipt files.
   632	16. Unit tests perform no network calls.
   633	17. No new module imports `haxjobs.agent`.
   634	
   635	Do not snapshot complete natural-language output.
   636	
   637	**Verify:**
   638	
   639	```bash
   640	PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run python3 -m pytest -q tests/test_stage0_job_review.py
   641	```
   642	
   643	Expected: all Stage 0 tests pass.
   644	
   645	### Step 9: Create the private backend-career fixture
   646	
   647	Read the real local profile and selected evidence sources from the operator-provided main checkout paths. Do not copy contact data, credentials, raw documents, or private paths.
   648	
   649	Write only `state/experiments/fixtures/backend-career.json` inside the executor worktree. It stays ignored.
   650	
   651	Validate it with the same Pydantic contract as the synthetic test fixture.
   652	
   653	Create a SHA-256 hash and record only that hash and fixture version in later tracked reports.
   654	
   655	**Verify:**
   656	
   657	```bash
   658	PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run python3 - <<'PY'
   659	from pathlib import Path
   660	from haxjobs.employment.fixtures import load_career_fixture
   661	p = Path("state/experiments/fixtures/backend-career.json")
   662	fixture = load_career_fixture(p)
   663	assert fixture
   664	print("private career fixture: PASS")
   665	PY
   666	git check-ignore state/experiments/fixtures/backend-career.json
   667	```
   668	
   669	Expected: validation passes and `git check-ignore` prints the path.
   670	
   671	### Step 10: Run the live Stage 0 experiment in the decided order
   672	
   673	Run Job 49 first:
   674	
   675	```bash
   676	PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run haxjobs experiment review-job --job 49 --live
   677	```
   678	
   679	Complete its generated `review.md` using the rubric.
   680	
   681	Required broad result:
   682	
   683	- identifies internal IT support rather than backend or AI engineering
   684	- uses actual supplied responsibilities
   685	- does not overvalue AI, automation, Azure, or Bash keywords
   686	- keeps sponsorship unknown
   687	- gives a clear probably-not-worth-the-time recommendation
   688	- sounds conversational rather than like an ATS scorecard
   689	- grounds every material claim in supplied context
   690	
   691	Then run Job 328:
   692	
   693	```bash
   694	PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run haxjobs experiment review-job --job 328 --live
   695	```
   696	
   697	Complete its review.
   698	
   699	Required broad result:
   700	
   701	- notices the evidence is only a title and URL stub
   702	- does not repeat hidden old-evaluation claims
   703	- refuses a confident fit judgement
   704	- names source inspection as the next useful check
   705

   707	
   708	**STOP:** if Job 49 still fails the grounding control after one correction, Job 328 does not expose the missing-source problem, the provider is unavailable, or either run lacks a complete receipt and human review. Stage 1 must not start.

   632	16. Unit tests perform no network calls.
   633	17. No new module imports `haxjobs.agent`.
   634	
   635	Do not snapshot complete natural-language output.
   636	
   637	**Verify:**
   638	
   639	```bash
   640	PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run python3 -m pytest -q tests/test_stage0_job_review.py
   641	```
   642	
   643	Expected: all Stage 0 tests pass.
   644	
   645	### Step 9: Create the private backend-career fixture
   646	
   647	Read the real local profile and selected evidence sources from the operator-provided main checkout paths. Do not copy contact data, credentials, raw documents, or private paths.
   648	
   649	Write only `state/experiments/fixtures/backend-career.json` inside the executor worktree. It stays ignored.
   650	
   651	Validate it with the same Pydantic contract as the synthetic test fixture.
   652	
   653	Create a SHA-256 hash and record only that hash and fixture version in later tracked reports.
   654	
   655	**Verify:**
   656	
   657	```bash
   658	PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run python3 - <<'PY'
   659	from pathlib import Path
   660	from haxjobs.employment.fixtures import load_career_fixture
   661	p = Path("state/experiments/fixtures/backend-career.json")
   662	fixture = load_career_fixture(p)
   663	assert fixture
   664	print("private career fixture: PASS")
   665	PY
   666	git check-ignore state/experiments/fixtures/backend-career.json
   667	```
   668	
   669	Expected: validation passes and `git check-ignore` prints the path.
   670	
   671	### Step 10: Run the live Stage 0 experiment in the decided order
   672	
   673	Run Job 49 first:
   674	
   675	```bash
   676	PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run haxjobs experiment review-job --job 49 --live
   677	```
   678	
   679	Complete its generated `review.md` using the rubric.
   680	
   681	Required broad result:
   682	
   683	- identifies internal IT support rather than backend or AI engineering
   684	- uses actual supplied responsibilities
   685	- does not overvalue AI, automation, Azure, or Bash keywords
   686	- keeps sponsorship unknown
   687	- gives a clear probably-not-worth-the-time recommendation
   688	- sounds conversational rather than like an ATS scorecard
   689	- grounds every material claim in supplied context
   690	
   691	Then run Job 328:
   692	
   693	```bash
   694	PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run haxjobs experiment review-job --job 328 --live
   695	```
   696	
   697	Complete its review.
   698	
   699	Required broad result:
   700	
   701	- notices the evidence is only a title and URL stub
   702	- does not repeat hidden old-evaluation claims
   703	- refuses a confident fit judgement
   704	- names source inspection as the next useful check
   705	
   706	One prompt or context correction is allowed only when the failure is explainable, recorded, and followed by rerunning both fixtures. Do not add tools.
   707	
   708	**STOP:** if Job 49 still fails the grounding control after one correction, Job 328 does not expose the missing-source problem, the provider is unavailable, or either run lacks a complete receipt and human review. Stage 1 must not start.
   709	
   710	### Step 11: Create the current-state diagram
   711

   713

   717	- `diagram/003-stage0-observed-job-review.png`
   718

   721	1. CLI experiment
   722	2. employment context
   723	3. no-tool agent core
   724	4. model boundary

   726	
   727	Show `No tools in Stage 0` clearly inside the agent-core group. Do not show sessions, databases, compaction, sub-agents, source inspection, company watches, or future workers.
   728	
   729	Keep file paths out of nodes. Keep the diagram under 35 cells. Use thick group arrows, destination colours, and import-safe XML.
   730	
   731	Update `diagram/README.md` with source, PNG, implementation report, and one-sentence current-state description.

     1	"""HaxJobs CLI."""
     2	import argparse
     3	import sys
     4	
     5	
     6	def _csv(value: str | None) -> list[str] | None:
     7	    if not value:
     8	        return None
     9	    return [item.strip() for item in value.split(",") if item.strip()]
    10	
    11	
    12	def cmd_start(args):
    13	    """Start the HaxJobs server."""
    14	    from haxjobs.server.main import run
    15	    run(host=args.host, port=args.port, open_browser=not args.no_browser, reload=args.reload)
    16	
    17	
    18	def cmd_agent_ask(args):
    19	    """Ask the native HaxJobs agent a question from the terminal."""
    20	    from haxjobs.agent import Agent, build_system_prompt, load_identity
    21	
    22	    prompt = " ".join(args.prompt).strip()
    23	    if not prompt and not sys.stdin.isatty():
    24	        prompt = sys.stdin.read().strip()
    25	    if not prompt:
    26	        raise SystemExit("Provide a prompt or pipe one on stdin.")
    27	
    28	    system = args.system
    29	    if not args.plain:
    30	        system = build_system_prompt(load_identity(), context_files=system or "")
    31	
    32	    tools = _csv(args.tools)
    33	    if tools:
    34	        text = Agent(tools=tools).run_with_tools(
    35	            prompt,
    36	            system=system,
    37	            max_turns=args.max_turns,
    38	        )
    39	    else:
    40	        text = Agent().run(prompt, system=system)
    41	    print(text)
    42	
    43	
    44	def cmd_dev_reset(args):
    45	    """Reset onboarding state for development — keeps provider config."""
    46	    from pathlib import Path
    47	    from urllib import request
    48	    from haxjobs.config import STATE_DIR
    49	
    50	    # Clear project runtime state
    51	    removed = []
    52	    if STATE_DIR.exists():
    53	        for p in sorted(STATE_DIR.glob("*")):
    54	            if p.name == "haxjobs.db":
    55	                continue  # ponytail: preserve DB, just clear profile
    56	            if p.is_file():
    57	                p.unlink()
    58	            elif p.is_dir():
    59	                import shutil
    60	                shutil.rmtree(p)
    61	            removed.append(p.name)
    62	    if removed:
    63	        print(f"State reset: {', '.join(removed)}")
    64	    else:
    65	        print("State: nothing to clear")
    66	
    67	    # Also clear in-memory session if server is running
    68	    try:
    69	        req = request.Request(
    70	            f"http://{args.host}:{args.port}/api/onboarding/reset",
    71	            method="POST",
    72	        )
    73	        request.urlopen(req, timeout=2)
    74	        print("Server session: cleared")
    75	    except Exception:
    76	        try:
    77	            request.urlopen(
    78	                f"http://{args.host}:{args.port}/api/onboarding/reset",
    79	                timeout=2,
    80	            )
    81	            print("Server session: cleared")
    82	        except Exception:
    83	            print("Server session: not running (run: haxjobs dev restart)")
    84	
    85	
    86	def cmd_dev_status(args):
    87	    """Show current onboarding state."""
    88	    from pathlib import Path
    89	    from haxjobs.features.onboarding.service import load_profile
    90	
    91	    print(f"Profile file : {'exists' if load_profile() else 'none'}")
    92	
    93	    try:
    94	        from urllib import request
    95	        import json
    96	        req = request.Request(f"http://{args.host}:{args.port}/api/onboarding/status")
    97	        resp = request.urlopen(req, timeout=2)
    98	        data = json.loads(resp.read())
    99	        print(f"Server status: {data.get('stage', 'unknown')}")
   100	
   101	        resp = request.urlopen(f"http://{args.host}:{args.port}/api/discovery/status", timeout=2)
   102	        discovery = json.loads(resp.read())
   103	        connected = isinstance(discovery.get("running"), bool) and isinstance(discovery.get("scrapers"), list)
   104	        print(f"Discovery API: {'connected' if connected else 'stale'}")
   105	    except Exception:
   106	        print("Server status: not running")
   107	
   108	
   109	def cmd_dev_restart(args):
   110	    """Kill running dev server and start a fresh one with reload."""
   111	    import os, signal, subprocess, sys
   112	    from pathlib import Path
   113	
   114	    # Kill existing
   115	    killed = False
   116	    for line in os.popen(f"ss -tlnp 'sport = :{args.port}'").readlines():
   117	        if "pid=" in line:
   118	            import re
   119	            m = re.search(r"pid=(\d+)", line)
   120	            if m:
   121	                os.kill(int(m.group(1)), signal.SIGTERM)
   122	                killed = True
   123	                print(f"Killed pid {m.group(1)}")
   124	
   125	    # Reset filesystem state (skip API call since we just killed the server)
   126	    from haxjobs.config import STATE_DIR
   127	    removed = []
   128	    if STATE_DIR.exists():
   129	        for p in sorted(STATE_DIR.glob("*")):
   130	            if p.name == "haxjobs.db":
   131	                continue
   132	            if p.is_file():
   133	                p.unlink()
   134	            elif p.is_dir():
   135	                import shutil
   136	                shutil.rmtree(p)
   137	            removed.append(p.name)
   138	    if removed:
   139	        print(f"State reset: {', '.join(removed)}")
   140	
   141	    # Start fresh
   142	    print("Starting dev server…")
   143	    os.chdir(args.project_root or os.getcwd())
   144	    subprocess.Popen(
   145	        [
   146	            sys.executable,
   147	            "-m",
   148	            "haxjobs.server.main",
   149	            "--no-browser",
   150	            "--reload",
   151	            "--host",
   152	            args.host,
   153	            "--port",
   154	            str(args.port),
   155	        ],
   156	        env={**os.environ, "PYTHONPATH": "src:."},
   157	        stdout=subprocess.DEVNULL,
   158	        stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL,
   159	    )
   160	    print(f"Server: http://{args.host}:{args.port}")
   161	    print(f"Onboarding: http://{args.host}:{args.port}/onboarding")
   162	
   163	
   164	def main(argv: list[str] | None = None):
   165	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
   166	        prog="haxjobs", description="Self-hosted job search platform"
   167	    )
   168	    sub = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command")
   169	
   170	    start = sub.add_parser("start", help="Start the server")
   171	    start.add_argument("--host", default="127.0.0.1")
   172	    start.add_argument("--port", type=int, default=8241)
   173	    start.add_argument("--no-browser", action="store_true", help="Don't open browser")
   174	    start.add_argument("--reload", action="store_true", help="Reload backend on code changes")
   175	    start.set_defaults(func=cmd_start)
   176	
   177	    agent = sub.add_parser("agent", help="Use the native HaxJobs agent")
   178	    agent_sub = agent.add_subparsers(dest="agent_command")
   179	    ask = agent_sub.add_parser("ask", help="Ask the agent a question")
   180	    ask.add_argument("prompt", nargs="*", help="Prompt text; stdin is used if omitted")
   181	    ask.add_argument("--system", default=None, help="Extra system/context text")
   182	    ask.add_argument("--plain", action="store_true", help="Skip HaxJobs identity prompt")
   183	    ask.add_argument("--tools", help="Comma-separated tools: web_search,fetch_page,db_query")
   184	    ask.add_argument("--max-turns", type=int, default=5)
   185	    ask.set_defaults(func=cmd_agent_ask)
   186	
   187	    dev = sub.add_parser("dev", help="Development utilities")
   188	    dev.add_argument("--host", default="127.0.0.1", help="Server host")
   189	    dev.add_argument("--port", type=int, default=8241, help="Server port")
   190	    dev_sub = dev.add_subparsers(dest="dev_command")
   191	
   192	    reset_cmd = dev_sub.add_parser("reset", help="Reset onboarding state (keeps provider config)")
   193	    reset_cmd.set_defaults(func=cmd_dev_reset)
   194	
   195	    status_cmd = dev_sub.add_parser("status", help="Show onboarding state")
   196	    status_cmd.set_defaults(func=cmd_dev_status)
   197	
   198	    restart_cmd = dev_sub.add_parser("restart", help="Kill current server, reset state, start fresh")
   199	    restart_cmd.add_argument("--project-root", default=None, help="Project directory")
   200	    restart_cmd.set_defaults(func=cmd_dev_restart)
   201	
   202	    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
   203	    if not hasattr(args, "func"):
   204	        parser.print_help()
   205	        sys.exit(0)
   206	    args.func(args)
   207	
   208	
   209	if __name__ == "__main__":
   210	    main()
     1	---
     2	name: clean-drawio
     3	description: Build clean grouped/swimlane draw.io diagrams for HaxJobs architecture. Use when Arinze asks for architecture diagrams, pipeline diagrams, file maps, or any diagram for this project.
     4	---
     5	
     6	# Clean Draw.io Architecture Diagrams
     7	
     8	Arinze's preference for draw.io architecture diagrams. Rebuild any diagram that has too many individual nodes, file listings inside nodes, or inconsistent layout.
     9	
    10	## Rules
    11	
    12	### Structure
    13	
    14	- **Groups, not nodes.** Use swimlane containers (`swimlane`) as the primary visual unit. Each major phase/zone is its own swimlane. Small nodes live inside swimlanes — they should have only the role name, never file paths or detail.
    15	- **5-7 groups max.** For pipeline: 5 groups (Discover, Store, Evaluate, Build Pack, Output). For file architecture: 6 groups (Config, Discovery, Database+State, Processing, Dashboard+API, Output).
    16	- **Minimal node text.** Inside a swimlane, nodes say things like "Job Sources", "Scrapers", "Fit Evaluation" — not "discovery/lever_scraper.sh calls Lever API with curl".
    17	- **Thick inter-group arrows.** Edges between swimlanes use `strokeWidth=2` or `strokeWidth=3` and match the destination group's color. No individual edges between tiny internal nodes.
    18	
    19	### Color scheme
    20	
    21	| Role | fillColor | strokeColor |
    22	|---|---|---|
    23	| Discovery / UI surfaces | `#dae8fc` (swimlane), `#e3f2fd` (node) | `#6c8ebf` |
    24	| Database / State | `#fff2cc` (swimlane), `#fff9e6` (node) | `#d6b656` |
    25	| Processing / Logic | `#e1d5e7` (swimlane), `#f3e8f7` (node) | `#9673a6` |
    26	| Manual / Gated | `#f8cecc` (swimlane), `#fde8e8` (node) | `#b85450` |
    27	| Output / Artifacts | `#d5e8d4` (swimlane), `#e8f5e9` (node) | `...

   129	        for p in sorted(STATE_DIR.glob("*")):
   130	            if p.name == "haxjobs.db":
   131	                continue
   132	            if p.is_file():
   133	                p.unlink()
   134	            elif p.is_dir():
   135	                import shutil
   136	                shutil.rmtree(p)
   137	            removed.append(p.name)
   138	    if removed:
   139	        print(f"State reset: {', '.join(removed)}")
   140	
   141	    # Start fresh
   142	    print("Starting dev server…")
   143	    os.chdir(args.project_root or os.getcwd())
   144	    subprocess.Popen(
   145	        [
   146	            sys.executable,
   147	            "-m",
   148	            "haxjobs.server.main",
   149	            "--no-browser",
   150	            "--reload",
   151	            "--host",
   152	            args.host,
   153	            "--port",
   154	            str(args.port),
   155	        ],
   156	        env={**os.environ, "PYTHONPATH": "src:."},
   157	        stdout=subprocess.DEVNULL,
   158	        stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL,
   159	    )
   160	    print(f"Server: http://{args.host}:{args.port}")
   161	    print(f"Onboarding: http://{args.host}:{args.port}/onboarding")
   162	
   163	
   164	def main(argv: list[str] | None = None):
   165	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
   166	        prog="haxjobs", description="Self-hosted job search platform"
   167	    )
   168	    sub = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command")
   169	
   170	    start = sub.add_parser("start", help="Start the server")
   171	    start.add_argument("--host", default="127.0.0.1")
   172	    start.add_argument("--port", type=int, default=8241)
   173	    start.add_argument("--no-browser", action="store_true", help="Don't open browser")
   174	    start.add_argument("--reload", action="store_true", help="Reload backend on code changes")
   175	    start.set_defaults(func=cmd_start)
   176	
   177	    agent = sub.add_parser("agent", help="Use the native HaxJobs agent")
   178	    agent_sub = agent.add_subparsers(dest="agent_command")
   179	    ask = agent_sub.add_parser("ask", help="Ask the agent a question")
   180	    ask.add_argument("prompt", nargs="*", help="Prompt text; stdin is used if omitted")
   181	    ask.add_argument("--system", default=None, help="Extra system/context text")
   182	    ask.add_argument("--plain", action="store_true", help="Skip HaxJobs identity prompt")
   183	    ask.add_argument("--tools", help="Comma-separated tools: web_search,fetch_page,db_query")
   184	    ask.add_argument("--max-turns", type=int, default=5)
   185	    ask.set_defaults(func=cmd_agent_ask)
   186	
   187	    dev = sub.add_parser("dev", help="Development utilities")
   188	    dev.add_argument("--host", default="127.0.0.1", help="Server host")
   189	    dev.add_argument("--port", type=int, default=8241, help="Server port")
   190	    dev_sub = dev.add_subparsers(dest="dev_command")
   191	
   192	    reset_cmd = dev_sub.add_parser("reset", help="Reset onboarding state (keeps provider config)")
   193	    reset_cmd.set_defaults(func=cmd_dev_reset)
   194	
   195	    status_cmd = dev_sub.add_parser("status", help="Show onboarding state")
   196	    status_cmd.set_defaults(func=cmd_dev_status)
   197	
   198	    restart_cmd = dev_sub.add_parser("restart", help="Kill current server, reset state, start fresh")
   199	    restart_cmd.add_argument("--project-root", default=None, help="Project directory")
   200	    restart_cmd.set_defaults(func=cmd_dev_restart)
   201	
   202	    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
   203	    if not hasattr(args, "func"):
   204	        parser.print_help()
   205	        sys.exit(0)
   206	    args.func(args)
   207	
   208	
   209	if __name__ == "__main__":
   210	    main()
     1	---
     2	name: clean-drawio
     3	description: Build clean grouped/swimlane draw.io diagrams for HaxJobs architecture. Use when Arinze asks for architecture diagrams, pipeline diagrams, file maps, or any diagram for this project.
     4	---
     5	
     6	# Clean Draw.io Architecture Diagrams
     7	
     8	Arinze's preference for draw.io architecture diagrams. Rebuild any diagram that has too many individual nodes, file listings inside nodes, or inconsistent layout.
     9	
    10	## Rules
    11	
    12	### Structure
    13	
    14	- **Groups, not nodes.** Use swimlane containers (`swimlane`) as the primary visual unit. Each major phase/zone is its own swimlane. Small nodes live inside swimlanes — they should have only the role name, never file paths or detail.
    15	- **5-7 groups max.** For pipeline: 5 groups (Discover, Store, Evaluate, Build Pack, Output). For file architecture: 6 groups (Config, Discovery, Database+State, Processing, Dashboard+API, Output).
    16	- **Minimal node text.** Inside a swimlane, nodes say things like "Job Sources", "Scrapers", "Fit Evaluation" — not "discovery/lever_scraper.sh calls Lever API with curl".
    17	- **Thick inter-group arrows.** Edges between swimlanes use `strokeWidth=2` or `strokeWidth=3` and match the destination group's color. No individual edges between tiny internal nodes.
    18	
    19	### Color scheme
    20	
    21	| Role | fillColor | strokeColor |
    22	|---|---|---|
    23	| Discovery / UI surfaces | `#dae8fc` (swimlane), `#e3f2fd` (node) | `#6c8ebf` |
    24	| Database / State | `#fff2cc` (swimlane), `#fff9e6` (node) | `#d6b656` |
    25	| Processing / Logic | `#e1d5e7` (swimlane), `#f3e8f7` (node) | `#9673a6` |
    26	| Manual / Gated | `#f8cecc` (swimlane), `#fde8e8` (node) | `#b85450` |
    27	| Output / Artifacts | `#d5e8d4` (swimlane), `#e8f5e9` (node) | `...

710	### Step 11: Create the current-state diagram
   711	
   712	Use `.agents/skills/clean-drawio/SKILL.md`.
   713	
   714	Create:
   715	
   716	- `diagram/003-stage0-observed-job-review.drawio`
   717	- `diagram/003-stage0-observed-job-review.png`
   718	
   719	The diagram shows only the implemented end state, with five groups:
   720	
   721	1. CLI experiment
   722	2. employment context
   723	3. no-tool agent core
   724	4. model boundary
   725	5. local artifacts and verification
   726	
   727	Show `No tools in Stage 0` clearly inside the agent-core group. Do not show sessions, databases, compaction, sub-agents, source inspection, company watches, or future workers.
   728	
   729	Keep file paths out of nodes. Keep the diagram under 35 cells. Use thick group arrows, destination colours, and import-safe XML.
   730	
   731	Update `diagram/README.md` with source, PNG, implementation report, and one-sentence current-state description.
   732	
   733	**Verify:**
   734	
   735	```bash
   736	python3 - <<'PY'
   737	from pathlib import Path
   738	import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
   739	p = Path("diagram/003-stage0-observed-job-review.drawio")
   740	root = ET.parse(p).getroot()
   741	cells = root.findall(".//mxCell")
   742	edges = [cell for cell in cells if cell.get("edge") == "1"]
   743	assert len(cells) <= 35, len(cells)
   744	assert all(edge.find("mxGeometry") is not None for edge in edges)
   745	print({"cells": len(cells), "edges": len(edges)})
   746	PY
   747	drawio --export --format png \
   748	  --output /tmp/haxjobs-stage0-diagram.png \
   749	  diagram/003-stage0-observed-job-review.drawio
   750	test -s /tmp/haxjobs-stage0-diagram.png
   751	test -s diagram/003-stage0-observed-job-review.png
   752	```
   753	
   754	Expected: XML parses, cell count is within the limit, every edge has geometry, and both PNGs are non-empty.
   755	
   756	### Step 12: Write the implementation evidence report
   757	
   758	Create `docs/implementation-reports/001-stage0-observed-job-review.md`.
   759	
   760	Use these exact headings:
   761	
   762	```text
   763	# Plan 001 implementation report
   764	## Attestation
   765	## Baseline and scope
   766	## What changed
   767	## Implemented call path
   768	## Manual CLI run
   769	## Automated verification
   770	## Live fixture evidence
   771	## Diagram verification
   772	## DeepSeek Flash review ledger
   773	## Deferred work
   774	## Residual risks
   775	## Deliverable manifest
   776	```
   777	
   778	The report must contain:
   779	
   780	- baseline SHA and candidate SHA
   781	- exact executor model ID and statement that no fallback or implementation delegation occurred
   782	- changed-file list
   783	- final call path with exact file and symbol names
   784	- fake and live manual commands
   785	- concise command outputs with exit codes
   786	- Job 49 and Job 328 run IDs, fixture hashes, prompt version, model, stop reason, and rubric outcome
   787	- safe bounded answer excerpts only when they contain no private data
   788	- no raw profile, complete prompt, transcript, fetched page, credential, or header
   789	- diagram source and PNG links
   790	- reviewer findings and resolutions
   791	- explicit deferrals
   792	- SHA-256 hashes for the report, draw.io source, and PNG in the final manifest
   793	
   794	The report is the detailed companion document for the diagram.
   795	
   796	### Step 13: Run full plan verification
   797	
   798	```bash
   799	uv lock --check
   800	PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run python3 -m pytest -q tests/
   801	PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run python3 -m py_compile \
   802	  $(find src/haxjobs/model src/haxjobs/agent_core \
   803	  src/haxjobs/employment src/haxjobs/interfaces tests -name '*.py')
   804	PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run haxjobs experiment review-job --help
   805	PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run haxjobs experiment review-job --job 49 --fake
   806	git diff --check
   807	git status --short
   808	```
   809	
   810	Expected:
   811	
   812	- every command exits 0
   813	- fake run produces a complete local receipt
   814	- only in-scope tracked files are changed
   815	- private fixtures and run artifacts do not appear in status
   816	
   817	Commit the candidate. Do not push.
   818	
   819	## DeepSeek v4 Flash review round
   820	
   821	Freeze the candidate commit and dispatch three fresh, independent `deepseek-v4-flash` reviewers. Each receives the same plan, commit range, discussion decisions, and evidence report. They do not receive each other's findings.
   822	
   823	### Reviewer A: architecture and scope
   824	
   825	Check:
   826	
   827	- four-layer separation
   828	- no imports from `haxjobs.agent`
   829	- one provider boundary
   830	- domain-free agent core
   831	- employment rules only in employment layer
   832	- thin CLI
   833	- zero tools
   834	- no speculative framework, session, registry, plugin, workflow, database, or UI work
   835	- every diff hunk maps to a plan step
   836	
   837	### Reviewer B: correctness, safety, and tests
   838	
   839	Check:
   840	
   841	- Pydantic validation at boundaries
   842	- safe provider failures
   843	- exact one-call behavior
   844	- event ordering
   845	- observer failure isolation
   846	- redaction
   847	- local file permissions
   848	- no credential or private-data leakage
   849	- fake tests use no network
   850	- all verification claims match actual command output
   851	
   852	### Reviewer C: deliverables and manual use
   853	
   854	Check:
   855	
   856	- `haxjobs experiment review-job --help`
   857	- fake and live command documentation
   858	- report completeness
   859	- diagram matches implemented current state
   860	- draw.io imports and exports
   861	- PNG is current and readable
   862	- `diagram/README.md` links work
   863	- a human can find and run the experiment without reading source
   864	
   865	Each reviewer returns findings only:
   866	
   867	```text
   868	ID
   869	Severity: blocker | major | minor | note
   870	Requirement
   871	Evidence
   872	Impact
   873	Required fix
   874	Verification
   875	```
   876	
   877	The advisor labels each finding accepted, rejected, or duplicate. The same Pro executor fixes every accepted blocker, major, and minor finding, adds regression coverage where useful, reruns the full plan verification, updates the report, and commits again.
   878	
   879	Run one final independent Flash re-review. At most two repair rounds. If an accepted blocker or major finding remains after round two, stop and replan.
   880	
   881	## Test plan
   882	
   883	The authoritative focused test file is `tests/test_stage0_job_review.py`.
   884	
   885	Tests are semantic and structural, not prose snapshots. They use the scripted fake. They never call the live provider.
   886	
   887	Manual live validation is separate and recorded in local receipts plus the tracked implementation report.
   888	
   889	## Done criteria
   890	
   891	All must hold:
   892	
   893	- [ ] Baseline was committed, clean, and plan SHA restamped before dispatch.
   894	- [ ] Exact `deepseek-v4-pro` implemented all code and deliverables.
   895	- [ ] Exact `deepseek-v4-flash` ran three independent review roles.
   896	- [ ] Pydantic v2 is a direct dependency and `uv lock --check` passes.
   897	- [ ] New code follows model, agent-core, employment, and interface boundaries.
   898	- [ ] No new module imports `haxjobs.agent`.
   899	- [ ] Stage 0 exposes zero tools and performs exactly one model call.
   900	- [ ] Job 49 and Job 328 fixtures preserve their evidence limits.
   901	- [ ] Synthetic tests contain no private data.
   902	- [ ] Private career fixture and run artifacts remain ignored.
   903	- [ ] Redacted JSONL contains no raw career/job/model text or credentials.
   904	- [ ] Exact selected context and transcript exist only in permission-restricted local artifacts.
   905	- [ ] `tests/test_stage0_job_review.py` passes without network.
   906	- [ ] All greenfield tests pass.
   907	- [ ] CLI help and fake run pass.
   908	- [ ] Two live runs exist and have completed human reviews.
   909	- [ ] Job 49 passes the control rubric.
   910	- [ ] Job 328 demonstrates the missing-source problem.
   911	- [ ] Draw.io XML parses, exports, and matches the current implementation.
   912	- [ ] PNG is non-empty and linked from `diagram/README.md`.
   913	- [ ] Implementation report contains commands, outputs, run evidence, reviewer ledger, and hashes.
   914	- [ ] Every accepted reviewer finding is resolved.
   915	- [ ] `git diff --check` passes.
   916	- [ ] Candidate worktree is clean after the final commit.
   917	- [ ] No out-of-scope file changed.
   918	
   919	## STOP conditions
   920	
   921	Stop and report without improvising if:
   922	
   923	- the baseline gate is not clean or this plan has not been restamped
   924	- exact DeepSeek Pro or Flash model IDs are unavailable
   925	- any in-scope file drifted from the restamped plan
   926	- implementing a step requires modifying an out-of-scope file
   927	- the new runtime would need to import or wrap `haxjobs.agent`
   928	- provider credentials would need to be copied into code, tests, logs, reports, or prompts
   929	- the private career fixture cannot be made truthful without tracked private data
   930	- a deterministic test needs live network access
   931	- observer or artifact failure cannot be represented without hiding the incomplete receipt
   932	- Job 49 fails grounding after one small prompt/context correction
   933	- Job 328 does not prove the source-information gap
   934	- a verification command fails twice after one reasonable correction
   935	- draw.io cannot import/export the simplified XML
   936	- an accepted blocker or major reviewer finding remains after two repair rounds
   937	
   938	## Maintenance notes
   939	
   940	- Stage 1 must extend this same core. It must not create another loop.

    71	
    72	The database stores exactly 5,000 characters and cuts off during the final candidate section. The content contains:
    73	
    74	### Company context
    75	
    76	- Trainline describes itself as a rail and coach travel platform operating through its mobile app, website, and business partners.
    77	- The listing claims more than 135 million monthly visits, £6.3 billion in annual ticket sales, more than 270 rail and coach partners, operations across more than 40 countries, and a team of more than 1,000 people.
    78	- Named office locations include London, Paris, Barcelona, Milan, Edinburgh, and Madrid.
    79	
    80	### Team and role
    81	
    82	- The role sits in Workplace Technology and the Tech Support team.
    83	- It is a Tier 2 internal IT-support role, not a software-engineering role.
    84	- The estate includes managed MacOS devices, Windows devices, Azure Active Directory, Windows Server, meeting-room equipment, and internal event support.
    85	
    86	### Stored responsibilities
    87	
    88	- Manage the ticket lifecycle, dashboards, workloads, and reporting.
    89	- Resolve incidents within service-level targets and escalate to Tier 3 teams.
    90	- Troubleshoot issues and perform root-cause analysis.
    91	- Progress service requests and support internal employees through the IT-service platform, collaboration tools, and an onsite Tech Bar.
    92	- Manage IT assets, hardware refreshes, inventory, secure disposal, onboarding, offboarding, laptop builds, access changes, and asset retrieval.
    93	- Support meeting-room audio and video equipment, remote administration, internal events, security checks, audits, technical documentation, and knowledge sharing.
    94	- Explore automation, AI, and ML for internal process improvements.
    95	
    96	### Stored technical requirements
    97	
    98	- Windows 11, MacOS, Android, and Linux familiarity.
    99	- JAMF, Intune, mobile-device management, Microsoft 365, Slack, and Atlassian products.
   100	- Active Directory, Azure Active Directory, SaaS systems, and cloud-migration knowledge.
   101	- PCI-DSS, ISO 20071 as written in the source, IT governance, TCP/IP, DNS, DHCP, LAN, WAN, and wireless networking.
   102	- Basic PowerShell, Windows command line, Apple scripting, Bash, security tools, IT-service systems, video-conferencing equipment, Autopilot, MFA, SSO, SAML, and cloud printing.
   103	- Desirable certifications include Microsoft Endpoint Administrator Associate, Apple or JAMF certificates, and CompTIA A+.

   495	
   496	Pi's JSONL session tree is good for conversation branching and replay.
   497	
   498	Career evidence, jobs, decisions, commitments, applications, and outcomes need their own durable records. A conversation transcript is not the career model.
   499	
   500	Stage 0 JSONL remains a run trace, not the final product database.
   501	
   502	## Do not build a plugin marketplace
   503	
   504	Pi's extension system supports custom tools, commands, providers, UI, resource loading, and event interception. HaxJobs has one developer and no proven third-party extension need.
   505	
   506	## Do not copy coding prompt text
   507	
   508	Keep Pi's assembly pattern, not its coding identity, file instructions, or project assumptions.
   509	
   510	## Do not treat Pi's design documents as shipped behaviour
   511	
   512	At the studied commit, parts of Pi's generic hooks, automatic compaction, crash recovery, and observability package remain planned or incomplete.
   513	
   514	We should copy verified runtime patterns and use design notes as ideas, not dependencies.
   515	
   516	# Proposed smallest architecture for Stage 0 and Stage 1
   517	
   518	```text
   519	experiment CLI
   520	    |
   521	    v
   522	EmploymentAgent
   523	    |-- context builder
   524	    |-- Hax instructions
   525	    |-- active capability selection
   526	    |-- AgentLoop
   527	    |      |-- ModelClient
   528	    |      |-- tool validation and dispatch
   529	    |      |-- lifecycle events
   530	    |
   531	    |-- JSONL event sink
   532	    |-- run result
   533	```
   534	
   535	Stage 0 active tools:
   536	
   537	```text
   538	[]
   539	```

   496	Pi's JSONL session tree is good for conversation branching and replay.
   497	
   498	Career evidence, jobs, decisions, commitments, applications, and outcomes need their own durable records. A conversation transcript is not the career model.
   499	
   500	Stage 0 JSONL remains a run trace, not the final product database.
   501	
   502	## Do not build a plugin marketplace
   503	
   504	Pi's extension system supports custom tools, commands, providers, UI, resource loading, and event interception. HaxJobs has one developer and no proven third-party extension need.
   505	
   506	## Do not copy coding prompt text
   507	
   508	Keep Pi's assembly pattern, not its coding identity, file instructions, or project assumptions.
   509	
   510	## Do not treat Pi's design documents as shipped behaviour
   511	
   512	At the studied commit, parts of Pi's generic hooks, automatic compaction, crash recovery, and observability package remain planned or incomplete.
   513	
   514	We should copy verified runtime patterns and use design notes as ideas, not dependencies.
   515	
   516	# Proposed smallest architecture for Stage 0 and Stage 1
   517	
   518	```text
   519	experiment CLI
   520	    |
   521	    v
   522	EmploymentAgent
   523	    |-- context builder
   524	    |-- Hax instructions
   525	    |-- active capability selection
   526	    |-- AgentLoop
   527	    |      |-- ModelClient
   528	    |      |-- tool validation and dispatch
   529	    |      |-- lifecycle events
   530	    |
   531	    |-- JSONL event sink
   532	    |-- run result
   533	```
